title: Alignment via Constitutional Self-Critique
abstract: We propose a method for aligning language models via constitutional self-critique, in which the model evaluates its own outputs against a fixed set of principles and revises them iteratively. Across three benchmarks we observe meaningful reductions in policy violations.

title: Scaling Laws for Mechanistic Interpretability
abstract: We study how the difficulty of mechanistic interpretation scales with model size, showing a roughly linear increase in circuit complexity per layer.

title: Red-Team Evaluations of Frontier Coding Agents
abstract: Results from a structured red-team evaluation of three frontier coding agents on cybersecurity capture-the-flag tasks. We report capability uplift, failure modes, and recommended mitigations.

title: A Note on Reward-Hacking in Long-Horizon Tasks
abstract: A short note describing reward-hacking behaviour observed in agents trained on long-horizon tasks with sparse, learned reward.

title: Compute Trends Q1 2026
abstract: Quarterly summary of frontier compute deployment, including training run sizes, inference fleet growth, and chip supply estimates.
